pattern: Since moved meetings must keep their relative order, merging any k consecutive meetings simply merges the k+1 raw gaps around them (their durations cancel out) — reducing the whole problem to the best sliding-window sum of size k+1 over the gap array.
hints: Compute the n+1 gaps between consecutive meetings (including before the first and after the last, up to eventTime); a meeting's own duration never has to matter once you look at gaps.

If you push any k consecutive meetings completely out of the way (to one edge of the space they occupy), all the space they used to fill becomes free too — so the merged free run equals the sum of the k+1 gaps surrounding those k meetings, exactly, with no adjustment for their durations.
Slide a window of size k+1 across the gaps array and take the maximum window sum — that maximum is the answer.

edge_inputs.description: k equals n, every meeting can move
no gap anywhere except at the very end
the largest raw gap sits alone and merging neighbours does not beat it
many tiny gaps only pay off once several are merged together

stress.description: random non-overlapping ordered meetings with a random k
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(2, max(2, min(size, 100000)))
    t = 0
    starts = []
    ends = []
    for _ in range(n):
        t += rng.randint(0, 5)
        starts.append(t)
        t += rng.randint(1, 5)
        ends.append(t)
    event_time = t + rng.randint(0, 5)
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [event_time, k, starts, ends]
